note: seq=115: FIX backup.json clobber + run a real backup (--no-block so it cannot exceed the puller step timeout). atlas-backup-status (old, counts *.dump=0) was clobbering backup.json hourly -> disable its timer; pgcopy backup (writes .csv.gz + real backup.json) now OWNS it. Background-start pgcopy backup; its backup.json will then show real retained_dumps + restore_tested (or its captured errors).

steps.note: seq=115: stop backup.json clobber + background-run pgcopy backup.

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import subprocess as s; s.run(['systemctl','stop','atlas-backup-status.timer']); s.run(['systemctl','disable','atlas-backup-status.timer']); s.run(['systemctl','reset-failed','atlas-pgcopy-backup.service']); s.run(['systemctl','start','--no-block','atlas-pgcopy-backup.service']); print('clobber disabled; pgcopy backup started (background)')"
